summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrates hostname and home directory to hardcoded bare IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js issues an HTTP GET to a hardcoded bare IP (161.97.149.48) with the installer's hostname (os.hostname()) and home directory path (os.homedir()) embedded as query parameters, then writes the response bytes to downloaded-image.jpg inside the package directory. The destination uses plain HTTP, no certificate pinning, and no integrity check on the returned bytes. The package's self-description is internally inconsistent: the package name implies a UI component library, package.json's description claims a CLI image downloader, and the README documents a 'Simple Text Utils' API (capitalize/reverse/wordCount) that does not exist in index.js. The author field is the placeholder 'Your Name'. The combination of unsolicited install-time exfiltration of host/user identifiers to a bare-IP endpoint, attacker-controlled response bytes staged inside the installed package, and three mutually contradictory cover stories is the shape of a recon/beacon dropper, not a legitimate image utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
